arts.ttl: Preview: Crystal Palace vs. Ipswich Town - prediction, team news, lineups
arts.sum: Sports Mole previews Saturday's Premier League clash between Crystal Palace and Ipswich Town, including predictions, team news and possible lineups.

arts.ttl: Eddie the Eagle can fly v Ipswich - FPL tips & team of the week
arts.sum: With question marks around many of the top strikers this week, why not take a flyer on the likes of Eddie Nketiah and Beto, asks Thomas Woods.

arts.ttl: FA Cup quarter-final draw IN FULL as Fulham face Crystal Palace
arts.sum: Fulham will be hoping that they can join Palace in the quarter-final draw when they travel to Manchester United . The draw for the quarter-final will take place following the clash at Old Trafford with just one tie on Monday night left to play as Nottingham Forest host Ipswich Town.

arts.ttl: Jean-Philippe Mateta's admission on Ruben Amorim as Man Utd eye £50m transfer
arts.sum: Manchester United's striker search has seen Jean-Philippe Mateta's name come up, and the French striker had his say about Ruben Amorim's side after February's league clash

arts.ttl: Man United 'eye audacious summer move for Crystal Palace star'- as top target Viktor Gyokeres 'cools on Old Trafford transfer', with the club unlikely to qualify for Champions League
arts.sum: Mateta has been in inspired form for the Eagles, scoring 15 goals in 33 matches. His emergence has coincided with the arrival of Oliver Glasner.

arts.ttl: Ruben Amorim believes Jean-Philippe Mateta will solve two issues for Man Utd
arts.sum: Manchester United are set to be in the market for a new striker this summer and it's understood that Crystal Palace's Jean-Philippe Mateta has admirers at Old Trafford

arts.ttl: Team News: Ipswich Town vs. Bournemouth injury, suspension list, predicted XIs
arts.sum: Sports Mole rounds up all of the latest injury and suspension news ahead of Sunday's Premier League clash between Ipswich Town and Bournemouth.

arts.ttl: Ankle surgery rules Szmodics out of Ireland matches
arts.sum: "In terms of those who went off the pitch, Axel won’t be available for this weekend and Cameron Burgess is still being assessed," McKenna added. Town boss Kieran McKenna, whose side will bid for just their fourth league win of the season on Saturday at Crystal Palace, said: "Sam Szmodics has gone for an ankle operation, unfortunately.
